name: Kasha-Stuffed Cabbage with Sweet and Savory Tomato Sauce
recipeIngredient: 12 ounces cabbage leaves separated or medium-large
16 ounces no-salt-added whole tomatoes, canned in puree - coarsely chopped
14 1/2 ounces stewed red ripe tomatoes, canned
recipeInstructions:
Description: A meatless version of a classic dish. Cook a few extra cabbage leaves in case some tear.
1. Heat the oil over medium heat in a heavy large nonstick skillet. Stir in the onions. Sprinkle with sugar, thyme, ginger, allspice, and cayenne. Reduce the heat to medium-low and cook, stirring frequently for about 25 minutes or until onions are very tender and light golden. Stir in garlic and cook for 30 seconds or until fragrant. Transfer mixture to a bowl, cover, and set aside.
2. Wash and dry the skillet. In a small saucepan, bring broth and 1/4 cup water to boil over high heat. Beat egg in a small bowl. Stir kasha into the saucepan and heat until warm when held above it. Add the kasha mixture to the skillet and cook stirring constantly and scraping bottom of pan for 2-4 minutes or until grains are dry and separate and toasted smelling. Stir in half the onions and broth and return to boil. Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 25 minutes or until kasha is tender and liquid nearly absorbed. Transfer to a bowl and let cool.
3. Meanwhile, bring salted water to boil over high heat in a large pot. Add cabbage leaves 2-3 at a time, pressing them down with spoon and cook uncovered for 2-3 minutes or until pliable. Remove with tongs and drain between paper towels. Repeat with remaining leaves.
4. Place remaining onions in heavy nonreactive saucepan. Stir tomatoes with puree, stewed tomatoes, orange juice, and tomato paste to boil over medium-high heat. Reduce heat to medium-low and simmer stirring frequently for 15 minutes or until flavors are blended and sauce is thickened. Stir in salt and pepper.
5. Preheat oven to 375F. Spread about 1-1/2 cups sauce in a 13-by-9-by-2-inch baking dish.
6. Mix egg substitute (or 2 eggs), dill, remaining salt and pepper with cooled kasha.
7. To roll cabbage, remove core from bottom of leaf and trim end. Spoon scant 1/4-cup filling onto lower half of leaf and shape into thick sausage. Fold top over filling, fold sides, and roll up seam side down in the sauce. Repeat with remaining leaves and filling.
8. Spoon remaining sauce over rolls and cover with foil. Bake for 20 minutes. Uncover and bake another 20 minutes or until sauce is thick and bubbly. Sprinkle cabbage with remaining dill and serve with sauce and sour cream, if desired.
